Java example 0.
Goal: teach how to parse input with a loop.
The example uses a ArrayList and a List<Integer> value.
```java
static int processJava00(java.util.List<Integer> items) {
    int total = 0;
    for (int value : items) {
        if (value >= 0) total += value;
    }
    return total;
}
```
// Read the code from top to bottom.
The function receives data, skips invalid values, and returns one clear result.
A common mistake in this topic is NullPointerException.
Check the empty input case before trusting the code.
Keep names descriptive, keep steps small, and test one behavior at a time.

The example uses a ArrayList and a Map<String, Integer> value.
```java
static int processJava0136(java.util.List<Integer> items) {
    int total = 0;
    for (int value : items) {
        if (value >= 0) total += value;
    }
    return total;
}
```
// Read the code from top to bottom.
The function receives data, skips invalid values, and returns one clear result.
A common mistake in this topic is NullPointerException.
Check the empty input case before trusting the code.
Keep names descriptive, keep steps small, and test one behavior at a time.

```java
        if (value >= 0) total += value;
    }
    return total;
}
```

```java
static int processJava0768(java.util.List<Integer> items) {
    int total = 0;
    for (int value : items) {
        if (value >= 0) total += value;
    }
    return total;
}
```
// Read the code from top to bottom.
The function receives data, skips invalid values, and returns one clear result.
A common mistake in this topic is NullPointerException.
Check the empty input case before trusting the code.
Keep names descriptive, keep steps small, and test one behavior at a time.

```java
    for (int value : items) {
        if (value >= 0) total += value;
    }
    return total;
}
```

The function receives data, skips invalid values, and returns one clear result.
A common mistake in this topic is NullPointerException.

```java
}
```

The example uses a HashSet and a Optional<String> value.
```java
static int processJava01666(java.util.List<Integer> items) {
    int total = 0;
    for (int value : items) {
        if (value >= 0) total += value;
    }
    return total;
}
```
// Read the code from top to bottom.
The function receives data, skips invalid values, and returns one clear result.
A common mistake in this topic is NullPointerException.
Check the empty input case before trusting the code.
Keep names descriptive, keep steps small, and test one behavior at a time.
